surface.name: Qovery Console production path: cloud cluster + first application
surface.selection_basis: Qovery Get Started presents hosted Console/cloud paths on the customer cloud. Prefer Console + managed cloud cluster + deploy first application over the free local k3s learning path, which skips real cloud onboarding.

alternatives_considered.name: Test & Learn Locally (k3s)
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Local laptop cluster; policy prefers hosted Console/cloud onboarding.

alternatives_considered.name: Deploy with AI Agent
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer AI path; Console cloud remains the direct platform path with explicit cluster steps.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Qovery account, provision a managed cloud cluster, and deploy a first application until it is reachable.
assumed_prior_state: A cloud provider account (AWS/GCP/Azure/Scaleway) can be connected.
A Git repository or container image is available to deploy.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: After cluster creation, a first application deploy completes and the app is reachable via the Qovery-provided URL/endpoint.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Application status shows running/deployed and the public URL loads application content.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Cloud cluster guide is Step 1 then return to deploy first application; Production SaaS path targets deployed apps on managed Kubernetes.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Qovery first success is a running app on the managed cluster, not account creation alone.

prerequisites.requirement: Qovery account (free start, no credit card per Get Started).
Cloud provider account credentials for cluster creation (~30 minutes).

primary_path.action: Sign up for Qovery and open the Console.
Create a managed Kubernetes cluster on a chosen cloud provider and wait until ready.
details: Docs state cluster creation takes about 30 minutes and is done once.

primary_path.action: Create a project/environment and add a first application from Git or container image.
Open the application URL and confirm it loads.

friction_gates.description: Customer cloud account required for managed cluster.
